tables.name: Crypt Sounds
rows: Water dripping in a slow, deliberate rhythm
Stone grinding against stone, far off
A voice reciting names, none of them yours
Chittering that stops the moment you listen for it
Your own footsteps, half a second delayed
Nothing at all, which is somehow worse

tables.name: Crypt Debris
rows: A broken sword, hilt still warm
Coins fused together in a small heap
A journal, pages swollen shut with damp
A statue's hand, snapped off at the wrist
A door with no visible hinges or handle
Bones arranged too neatly to be an accident

sections.title: Setting
lines: The crypt flooded a generation ago; the lower levels are still wet.
Nobody who mapped this place agrees on how many levels there are.
Something down here is still being fed — by whom is the question.

scene.title: The Door Was Sealed From Inside
scene.body: The entrance to the crypt was bricked up from the inside, not the outside. You're standing in front of the gap someone else already made.
